reasoning: The CC4WSA has spiked recently, bringing the 10-25 value to 1,947,500. The implied individual weekly values show that recent weeks are extremely high (~1,964k) compared to 4 weeks prior (~1,927k). For the 11-02 resolution, the 4-week average will drop a low number and almost undoubtedly add a higher number from the currently elevated continuing claims (driven by hurricanes Helene/Milton and the Boeing strike). Thus, an increase by 11-02 is highly probable. By 11-25, this historically transient shock will likely have faded. Given that the pre-shock baseline in September was ~1,925k, the moving average is strongly expected to resolve back down below 1,947.5k by late November. By 01-24, transient noise will be entirely washed out, leaving us with the overall macro trend, making a figure above or below 1,947.5k roughly an even bet as the labor market continues a slow cooling but starts to feel the effects of Fed easing.
